mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% indicates strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite declining UNRATE, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) reflect robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved economic growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with HOUST above 975.0, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with INDPRO above 110.9051, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate monetary policy reacting to inflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods with CPIAUCSL > 215.132 and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k per month signals a tightening labor market that drives wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in higher rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of periods with PAYEMS > 137640.0, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) reflects strong household demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of periods with RSAFS above 384211.0, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of periods with GDPC1 above 11703.6, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
